select_when: Uncertainty can be reduced faster and cheaper through targeted tests than through longer debate, forecasting, or opinion escalation.
Each experiment can be tied to a clear hypothesis, success threshold, and downstream decision instead of being run for its own sake.
Reversible probes can inform larger irreversible commitments, reallocations, or product choices.
The team is stuck in argument about what is true, but a structured test could resolve the dispute.
avoid_when: Experimentation has become theater, generating activity, dashboards, or post-hoc learning language without changing any real decision.
Local test wins are being overgeneralized while the team ignores system interactions, long-term effects, or behavior changes outside the test window.

curation_notes.summary: Focused the model on hypothesis-linked tests, reversible probes, and real decision movement rather than on generic iteration language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style product-experimentation sprawl because the raw source is stronger on hypothesis discipline, anti-theater warnings, and system-aware test design.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch keeps Wave 4 observational only.
open_questions: The reasoning types are reviewed compression over a source that also leans strongly into systems and metacognitive support.

select_when.note: The raw strengths section is explicit on targeted tests, clear hypotheses, and reversible probes; review mainly compacts those bullets into routing triggers.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by explicit danger lines in the limitations section.

input_type.note: The raw model activates on testable uncertainty and disputed claims, but the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook produces hypothesis-linked tests that inform later decisions; the output label compresses that into a typed experiment artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Experimentation clearly uses causal and diagnostic reasoning, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
